truckdefensivew.description: A heavy, reinforced Dunne transport. Fitted with a heavier frame, the Leatherback is capable of enduring more punishment at the cost of initial acceleration.

truckmobilityc.name: R-5b “Sisyphus” Hauler
truckmobilityc.description: This variation of the standard R-5 Hauler is fitted with an improved suspension and axle system resulting in better overall handling. However, these improvements may not hold up under severe weather conditions.

truckmobilityw.name: Dunne Landrunner 12c
truckmobilityw.description: This standard Truck is fitted with rugged off-road treads, allowing for more efficient movement on rough terrain and conditions at the expense of maximum speed.

truckmultic.name: R-17 “Retiarius” Skirmisher
truckmultic.description: A truck fitted with an advanced rocket propulsion rack, the “Retiarius” webs the sky with deadly, screeching rockets shot at a high frequency over long distances. Holds sixteen rockets.

truckoffensivec.name: R-9 “Speartip” Escort
truckoffensivec.description: This standard Truck is fitted with Light Machinegun in place of the passenger seat. It’s well suited as an escort for convoys or lightly armoured operations.

truckw.name: Dunne Transport
truckw.description: A heavy-duty Warden truck used to mobilize troops and supplies.

unexplodedordnance.name: Unexploded Ordnance
unexplodedordnance.description: An old war artillery shell that failed to detonate. Etchings on the side indicate a powerful payload and should be handled carefully.

waterbucket.name: Water Bucket
waterbucket.description: A bucket that holds water. You can toss water onto fires to extinguish them.

watermine.name: E680-S Rudder Lock
watermine.description: Designed for underwater deployment, Sea Mines detonate upon contact with enemy vessels.

windsockt.name: Wind Sock
windsockt.description: A sturdy fabric cylinder mounted on a tripod that’s designed to help nearby operators determine the speed and direction of wind.

wood.name: Refined Materials
wood.description: Resource used for building advanced structures and producing special items.

workhammer.name: Hammer
workhammer.description: A tool used to build structures and assemble materials.

workwrench.name: Wrench
workwrench.description: A multipurpose tool. Can be used to dismantle mines/barbed wire and unlock vehicles.

wreckage.name: Wreckage
wreckage.description: Destroyed equipment reclaimed from the battlefield. Can be sorted into useful resources at a Refinery (using the right click context menu)

aircraftbomberc.name: V-1 Tzykalia
aircraftbomberc.description: A large, deadly bomber capable of delivering high volumes of explosive payloads. While the V-1 Tzykalia requires a proficient bombardier crew, this Velian-made aircraft has an infamous reputation overseas on the Katoma front. Facing minimal air-to-air resistance, a full squadron of Tzykalias led by seasoned captain and ace lodesman Calliope Bibas erased a large Estrellan fortress in the desert region of Sorra Blava off the map. This operation left Alliance presence in the west reeling, furthering Republic ambitions in the region.

aircraftbomberw.name: Tulka P4 Welkinrive
aircraftbomberw.description: A Nevish bomber that was developed in direct response to a lack of aerial heavy explosive support during Alliance operations. Having been repeatedly waylaid by Tzykalia bombers along the Sorovian coast, it became clear that the Sky Brigade needed to improve their armada. The Brigade commissioned a light and fuel-efficient bomber still capable of deploying catastrophic payloads.

aircraftdepot.name: Aircraft Depot
aircraftdepot.description: A large building used to store crates, vehicles, shippable structures, and aircraft.

aircraftdivec.name: Mergo-4 “Myrmidon”
aircraftdivec.description: This unique dive bomber quickly deposits small payloads with more precision than its larger, less mobile counterparts. Bombing first and asking questions later, “Myrmidons” often lead larger bombing operations. Its effective hit-and-run tactics soften larger targets or clear out emplacements that may pose a threat to less manoeuvrable aircraft or incoming convoys.

aircraftfighterc.name: Toxot-902 “Blind Silver”
aircraftfighterc.description: The Toxot-902 is a modern variant of the first warplane to ever see combat; it tears across battlefields laying down strafing machine gun support but excels in air-to-air dogfights. Easy to manufacture and field in large numbers, “Blind Silvers” often give the impression of a volley of arrows soaring overhead.

aircraftfighterw.name: Luminary Mk. II Harbinger
aircraftfighterw.description: The backbone of the Alliance Sky Brigade, the Harbinger excels at both strafing ground targets and dogfighting with its twin, front-facing machine guns. Not willing to be outclassed by crude Mesean engineering, Luminary—formerly a northern Caoivish industrial engineering firm—set to work on designing its Mk. I prototype. Sleek, aerodynamic, and so fast it screams, the Harbinger’s design is intended to give the Nevish Alliance a psychological edge on the battlefield as much as it provides air support.

aircraftparatrooperc.name: V-5b Pegasus
aircraftparatrooperc.description: A handful of these Velian-made aircraft are capable of depositing entire platoons of Colonial paratroopers deep behind Warden lines. In fact, these selfsame paratroopers are feared throughout the north for their commitment to night diving and opening their chute late enough that even an observant enemy might blink and miss their descent. While most paratroopers have a short lifespan, one trio that called themselves “The Furies” successfully completed over one hundred combat jumps into Caoivish territory. It’s believed The Furies are responsible for sabotaging countless Warden operations in the north and played a pivotal role in Legate Thea Maro’s “Operation Sunfall.”

aircraftparatrooperw.name: Rinnspeir Mk. I Zealot
aircraftparatrooperw.description: A recent addition to the Alliance Sky Brigade, the Zealot is a first-of-its-kind dropship out of Sorovia. Its large fuselage and cargo bay facilitate transporting entire paratrooper units and dropping them into the fray with ease. The Zealot is best paired with escorts, as it doesn’t boast much in the way of firepower, but once it deploys a retinue of Cloudrunners, it circles back to base to load up the next squad.

aircraftrunwayt1.name: Aircraft Runway (Tier 1)
aircraftrunwayt1.description: A stable runway that allows smaller aircraft to take off.

aircraftrunwayt2.name: Aircraft Runway (Tier 2)
aircraftrunwayt2.description: A stable runway that allows larger aircraft to take off.

aircraftscoutc.name: A51 Venti “Daedalus”
aircraftscoutc.description: Inspired by passenger seaplanes, the A51 is an extremely proficient scout plane. Dubbed “The Daedalus” by countless crews for its intrepid design, pontoons and foldable wheels give the flexibility to land, refuel, or observe enemy movements under various conditions unrivalled by more conventional aircraft.

aircraftscoutw.name: Luminary Mk. IV Herald
aircraftscoutw.description: The Mk. IV is a lighter Luminary aircraft designed for high and low-altitude intelligence gathering. Living up to its namesake, the Herald has been instrumental in thwarting several Colonial operations in the west. What it lacks in resilience and firepower, it more than makes up for in flexibility.

aircrafttorpedow.name: M925 Austringer Man-O-War
aircrafttorpedow.description: The M925 Edellian bomber is the Alliance Sky Brigade’s ace at air-to-sea combat. Featuring twin torpedo bays and a rear-mounted machine gun, the Man-O-War is a veritable threat to any ship’s hull or would-be assailants. The M925's unique capabilities overdelivered, as it was originally conceived as nothing more than a short-range coastal defence bomber. During the M925’s first operation off of the Katoman coast, a small squadron single-handedly sank a Titan. Her crew had no idea how their ship filled with seawater as torpedo after torpedo bored into the underside of her hull.

aircraftwaterw.name: Tulka I1.9 White Raven
aircraftwaterw.description: This unique Nevish fighter was specially designed to be launched from Mercy-class aircraft carriers, with the flexibility to operate in different environments. Fitted with several front-facing armaments, the White Raven is known to swarm enemy armadas out of nowhere. They soon find themselves careening out of the clouds.

ambulancec.name: R-12 - “Salus” Ambulance
ambulancec.description: The “Salus” Ambulance is efficient at transporting Critically Wounded Soldiers and carrying medical supplies.

ambulanceflamec.name: R-12b - “Salva” Flame Truck
ambulanceflamec.description: This simple variant of the “Salus” ambulance is fitted with a high-powered hose designed to quell wildfires.

ambulanceflamew.name: Dunne Dousing Engine 3r
ambulanceflamew.description: A simple variant of the Dunne Responder 3e that’s fitted with a high-powered hose designed to extinguish raging flames.

ambulancew.name: Dunne Responder 3e
ambulancew.description: The Responder Ambulance is efficient at transporting Critically Wounded Soldiers and carrying medical supplies.

ammunition_factory.name: Ammunition Factory
ammunition_factory.description: A factory for producing advanced ammo types such as large calibre shells, rockets, and flame ammo.

tripod_factory.name: Tripod Factory
tripod_factory.description: Produces various types of tripod weapons.

rocket_factory.name: Rocket Battery Workshop
rocket_factory.description: Produces various types of ammunition for rocket vehicles and structures.

armoredcar2multiw.name: O’Brien v.200 Squire
armoredcar2multiw.description: The second full iteration of Conor O’Brien’s storied Armoured Car designs, the Squire serves as a reinforced rocket platform. The squire—designed to play a support role for larger assault operations with its rocket battery platform—is easy to reposition and provides operators and nearby infantry with more protection than might be afforded by a standard utility vehicle.

armoredcaratw.name: O’Brien V.113 Gravekeeper
armoredcaratw.description: A slight variation of the V.110, the Gravekeeper comes fitted with an embedded Bonesaw launcher, transforming the humble armoured car into an effective indirect anti-armour vehicle.

armoredcarc.name: T3 “Xiphos”
armoredcarc.description: Colonial Armoured Cars are quick, well-rounded urban assault platforms. These anti-infantry vehicles are equipped with twin-barrelled machineguns.

armoredcarflamew.name: O’Brien V.130 Wild Jack
armoredcarflamew.description: While the Noble Firebrand Mk. XVII is a deadly flamethrower tank, a more efficient means of employing flame weapons was needed. Enter the Wild Jack. Named for the fiery idols made by children for Dead Harvest, the Wild Jack is a variation of the Highlander.

armoredcarmobilityw.name: O’Brien V.121 Highlander
armoredcarmobilityw.description: Fitted with all-terrain treads, the Highlander brings significant all-terrain mobility and performs especially well in snowy and mountainous environments.

armoredcaroffensivec.name: T5 “Percutio”
armoredcaroffensivec.description: This “Xiphos” variant is fitted with a high-powered anti-tank turret in place of the twin machine gun platform.

armoredcaroffensivew.name: O’Brien V.101 Freeman
armoredcaroffensivew.description: This early O’Brien variant, the V.101 Freeman is fitted with a 360 degree ballistics cannon turret at the expense of top speed.

armoredcartwinc.name: T8 “Gemini”
armoredcartwinc.description: Fitted with twin RPG launchers, the T8 employs hit-and-run assaults against enemy structures and emplacements.

armoredcartwinw.name: O’Brien V.190 Knave
armoredcartwinw.description: One of Conor O’Brien’s best traits was his ability to modernize and make use of older technology in his designs. The V.190 Knave is the perfect example of this philosophy. Fitted with a modified, outdated twin-grenade launcher turret, the Knave is a surprising combination of speed and subterfuge that quickly routs the enemy, leaving them befuddled.

armoredcarw.name: O’Brien V.110
armoredcarw.description: Warden Armoured Cars are quick, well-rounded urban assault platforms. These anti-infantry vehicles are equipped with twin-barrelled machineguns.

atpillbox.name: Anti-Tank Rifle Pillbox
atpillbox.description: This reinforced pillbox fires a 14.5mm anti-tank rifle at approaching enemy infantry and vehicles.

barbedwirespline.name: Barbed Wire
barbedwirespline.description: Used to slow down enemy infantry movement. Can be dismantled with a Wrench.

barbedwirewallspline.name: Barbed Wire Fence
barbedwirewallspline.description: Used to prevent enemy infantry movement through an area. This structure is difficult to destroy with conventional weapons and must be dismantled with a Wrench.

barge.name: BMS - Aquatipper
barge.description: A large shipping vessel, the Aquatipper is used to transport vehicles, equipment, and personnel over large bodies of water.

battletankatc.name: Lance-25 “Hasta”
battletankatc.description: A heavy Lance variation, the 25, or “Hasta”, was employed in the first siege on Brightwall, a city now colloquially referred to as, “The Blemish”. Bombarded by heavy, armoured resistance in northern Veli, Colonial tank regiments requested a heavy vehicle with more effective search and destroy capabilities. Fitted with a front-facing 94.5mm cannon, the “Hasta” is a more than capable tank destroyer.

battletankc.name: Lance-36
battletankc.description: A heavy-duty Battle Tank with thick armour plating and destructive firepower. The Lance is fitted with a 75mm turret and a front-facing 12.7mm anti-infantry machine gun. The heavy armour limits top speed, but in exchange it can take a lot of punishment.

battletankdefensivew.name: Flood Juggernaut Mk. VII
battletankdefensivew.description: The Juggernaut is a heavily armoured Flood variant fitted with a heavy flamethrower turret that fires an advanced adhesive propellant. Its shovel-like treads may hinder top speeds, but this beast is quite capable in extreme weather conditions.

battletankheavyartilleryc.name: Lance-46 “Sarissa”
battletankheavyartilleryc.description: This high-powered mobile artillery platform deploys a set of sturdy, mechanical legs to brace for the power of 150mm cannon fire. The “Sarissa” Lance variant is a highly custom and ingenious use of the Lance chassis that creates versatility in armour operations, reducing much of the overhead when transporting and deploying static artillery batteries.

battletankheavyartilleryw.name: Flood Mk. IX Stain
battletankheavyartilleryw.description: Built on an inverted Flood chassis, the Mk. IX Stain is a fully-featured mobile artillery platform. Once deployed, the Stain can fire a 150mm cannon over long-distances to pummel enemy positions with indirect fire.

battletankw.name: Flood Mk. I
battletankw.description: A heavy-duty Battle Tank with thick armour plating and destructive firepower. The Flood is fitted with a 75mm turret and a front-facing 12.7mm anti-infantry machine gun. The heavy armour limits top speed, but in exchange it can take a lot of punishment.

bicycle.name: Blumfield LK205
bicycle.description: A simple, old-style Blumfield bicycle. This is a smaller recreation model, designed for short-distance sprints.

bms_foreman_stacker.name: BMS Foreman Stacker
bms_foreman_stacker.description: The Foreman is an all-purpose, no-frills, easy to assemble crane. Perfect for hauling heavy loads in facilities where loading and offloading is commonplace.

bms_overseer_skyhauler.name: BMS - Overseer Sky-Hauler
bms_overseer_skyhauler.description: The star of the BMS arsenal of military-grade construction equipment, the Overseer Sky-Hauler makes several tonnes look like a feather, and enjoys a high level of mobility while deployed along heavy-duty rail lines.

borderbase.name: Border Base
borderbase.description: A small base used as a jump-off point for invading enemy regions. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

busc.name: R-15 - “Chariot”
busc.description: The “Chariot” is a transport vehicle used to shuttle personnel to the front line.

busw.name: Dunne Caravaner 2f
busw.description: The Caravaner is a transport vehicle used to shuttle personnel to the front line.

catwalk_bridge.name: Catwalk Bridge
catwalk_bridge.description: A raised walkway for creating paths above complex Facilities.

catwalk_platform.name: Catwalk Platform
catwalk_platform.description: A raised walkway for creating paths above complex Facilities.

catwalk_stairs.name: Catwalk Stairs
catwalk_stairs.description: Stairs that connect to raised Catwalks, which create paths above complex Facilities.

coal_refinery.name: Coal Refinery
coal_refinery.description: A processing plant that refines Coal into useful materials used in facility production and power.

coke_furnace.name: Coke Furnace
coke_furnace.description: A high powered furnace that brings Coal to high temperatures to separate out non-volatile Coke.

liquifier.name: Coal Liquefier
liquifier.description: Processing vats that refines Coal into Oil.

adv_liquifier.name: Advanced Coal Liquefier
adv_liquifier.description: Advanced processing vats that refine large amounts of coal into Heavy Oil.

coastalgun.name: Coastal Gun
coastalgun.description: An old coastal defence gun that has been reused for the defence of key locations against naval bombardment.

componentmine.name: Component Mine
componentmine.description: An old mine that automatically gathers Components using Fuel.

concretemixer.name: Concrete Mixer
concretemixer.description: A portable device that mixes various materials to form Concrete, which are used to build heavily fortified structures.

construction_vehicle.name: BMS - Universal Assembly Rig
construction_vehicle.description: A specialized vehicle designed by the Bassett Motor Society used in the construction of large structures.

constructionequipment.name: Construction Equipment
constructionequipment.description: An automated excavator that can be fuelled with Gravel to assist with construction projects.

constructionutility.name: BMS - Fabricator
constructionutility.description: An advanced variant of the Universal Assembly Rig, the BMS - Fabricator is fitted with a unique kit designed to handle advanced or specialized construction and excavation jobs.

constructionyard.name: Construction Yard
constructionyard.description: An area where certain structures can be built and prepared for shipping.

crane.name: BMS - Class 2 Mobile Auto-Crane
crane.description: The Bassett Motor Society’s Class 2 Mobile Auto-Crane is used to lift and reposition vehicles and very heavy equipment.

destroyertankflamew.name: Noble Firebrand Mk. XVII
destroyertankflamew.description: In response to the Legion embarking on aggressive northern offensives, the Firebrand is designed to conflagrate and eradicate their garrisons with ease. Built using the aggressive frame of the Widow, the Firebrand is a pure force of nature when paired with a secondary armoured escort.

destroyertankw.name: Noble Widow MK. XIV
destroyertankw.description: This deadly tank turns predator into prey. A tank Destroyer, the Noble Widow specializes in ambush tactics, waiting for its quarry and striking with destructive high-velocity shells.

diesel_power_plant.name: Diesel Power Plant
diesel_power_plant.description: A structure that generates power by burning Diesel.

petrol_power.name: Petrol Power Plant
petrol_power.description: A generator that burns petrol to generate more power.

coal.name: Coal Power Plant
coal.description: A generator that burns coal to generate power.

emplacedaircraftc.name: DAE 5b “Zeal”
emplacedaircraftc.description: A sturdy, stationary cannon for bombarding high-flying enemy aircraft with explosive shrapnel shells. The DAE 5b anti-aircraft emplacement has become infamous for walls of black smoke its payload leaves behind.

emplacedaircraftw.name: Leary AA-70 Bolas
emplacedaircraftw.description: A late entry into the Leary arsenal, the AA-70 Bolas was a direct response to a sudden uptick in Colonial aerial aggression. This emplaced cannon excels equally at both slowing and bringing down enemy aircraft.

emplacedatlargew.name: Huber Starbreaker 94.5mm
emplacedatlargew.description: Built to compliment other free-standing artillery pieces, the emplaced Huber Starbreaker launches 94.5mm shells over very long distances. A necessary tool for infantry to knock back armoured Colonial advances.

emplacedatw.name: Leary Shellbore 68mm
emplacedatw.description: A defensive emplacement with a 68mm Anti-tank cannon. This rudimentary weapon was once built to fit onto the hull of large naval vessels, but was eventually phased out and repurposed.

emplacedcannonlargec.name: DAE 2a-1 “Ruptura”
emplacedcannonlargec.description: Not to be outdone by its counterparts, the 2a-1 “Ruptura” is fitted with a 75mm cannon. Designed to aid in holding ground for longer durations, the “Ruptura” is a strong, efficient anti-armour tool.

emplacedheavyartilleryc.name: 50-500 “Thunderbolt” Cannon
emplacedheavyartilleryc.description: This heavy artillery cannon is designed to cripple enemy fortifications from an entrenched position. Its long heavy barrel gives the "Thunderbolt" outstanding range.

emplacedheavyartilleryw.name: Huber Exalt 150mm
emplacedheavyartilleryw.description: A heavy cannon designed to shatter the garrisons and fortifications of advancing forces. The Exalt is best utilized when emplaced into a defensive position to take advantage of its impressive range.

emplacedindirectc.name: DAE 1o-3 “Polybolos”
emplacedindirectc.description: To combat Caoivish ingenuity, the Meseans developed their own indirect RPG propulsion system. Dual launchers are fitted on a large emplacement platform to maximize coverage in an established position, rather than being relegated to a guerrilla warfare tool.

emplacedinfantryc.name: DAE 1b-2 “Serra”
emplacedinfantryc.description: Built like a saw blade, the DAE 1b-2 emplacement gun is fitted with three cascading machine gun turrets. What the "Serra" lacks in power, it makes up for in sheer rate of fire for a weapon of its size.

emplacedinfantryw.name: Leary Snare Trap 20
emplacedinfantryw.description: The Snare Trap is a repurposed anti-aircraft flak cannon used to fortify emplaced positions with twin anti-infantry machine guns.

emplacedlightartilleryw.name: Huber Lariat 120mm
emplacedlightartilleryw.description: A light artillery cannon designed to be a fixture in defensive fortifications. The Lariat sports a formidable long-range 120mm cannon designed to put immense pressure on enemy infantry.

emplacedmultic.name: DAE 3b-2 “Hades’ Net”
emplacedmultic.description: The DAE 3b-2 fires rockets over long distances from a safe, emplaced position. This unique weapon was given its name due to the screams heard across the battlefield during its first deployment, with some referring to the barrage as a “web of death.”

engineeringcenter.name: Engineering Center
engineeringcenter.description: A research facility where players can contribute towards the research of their faction's Tech Tree.

facilityfactoryaircraft.name: Aircraft Maintenance Factory
facilityfactoryaircraft.description: A factory for producing and repairing advanced aircraft parts.

aircraftstrategic.name: Strategic Maintenance Hangar
aircraftstrategic.description: A factory for producing and repairing heavy-lift strategic aircraft parts.

facilityfactorysmallarms.name: Infantry Kit Factory
facilityfactorysmallarms.description: A factory that specializes in manufacturing infantry uniforms. Modifications may be constructed to manufacture infantry arms and heavy ammunition.

specialweapons.name: Special-Issue Firearms Assembly
specialweapons.description: A collection of stations to facilitate the assembly of special-issue infantry weapons and explosives.

infantryammo.name: Small Arms Workshop
infantryammo.description: This workshop addition allows for manufacturing infantry carried explosives and various small arms ammo classes.

heavyammo.name: Heavy Munitions Foundry
heavyammo.description: A foundry wing that facilitates the manufacturing and assembly of heavy ammo classes employed by tanks and heavier infantry weapons.

facilitymineoilrig.name: Offshore Platform
facilitymineoilrig.description: Offshore Platforms are open-water extraction facilities used to extract various oils. A steady supply of processed Coal is required for certain operations, as extracting materials from the ocean floor is a complex process.

facilitytransfercrate.name: Crate Transfer Station
facilitytransfercrate.description: This facility is for storing crates of equipment manufactured at nearby factories.

facilityvehiclefactory3.name: Dry Dock
facilityvehiclefactory3.description: A floodable basin and construction platform that facilitates the assembly and repair of the largest naval vessels.

factory.name: Factory
factory.description: A manufacturing plant for producing military equipment.

field_modification_center.name: Field Modification Center
field_modification_center.description: A garage that facilitates vehicle modifications and upgrades.

fieldat2c.name: GA6 “Cestus”
fieldat2c.description: This simple, dual-barreled short-range field gun packs a punch. Its economic construction makes for fairly basic assembly at the cost of longevity. Meant for Anti-Tank support, the Cestus is best utilized in ambush tactics or woven between the lines of heavier armour.

fieldat2w.name: Duncan’s Coin 14.5mm
fieldat2w.description: This field gun is a capable, if somewhat flimsy, anti-tank weapon derived from cheap materials, crude processing, and utilizes dated technology. The affixed 14.5mm cannon may feel ancient by modern standards, but rest assured, it has no problems piercing modern armour.

fieldatc.name: AA-2 "Battering Ram"
fieldatc.description: The "Battering Ram" is a mobile Anti-Tank field gun firing 68mm armour-piercing rounds.

fieldatdamagew.name: Balfour Rampart 68mm
fieldatdamagew.description: The Rampart is a powerful field cannon capable of dealing devastating damage to all but the heaviest of armoured vehicles while providing its crew with comprehensive ballistic shielding.

fieldatw.name: Collins Cannon 68mm
fieldatw.description: The Collins Cannon is a mobile Anti-Tank field gun firing 68mm armour-piercing rounds.

fieldbridge.name: Field Bridge
fieldbridge.description: A makeshift bridge for transporting light equipment over a short distance. Will take damage when vehicles drive across.

fieldcannondamagec.name: 40-45 “Smelter”
fieldcannondamagec.description: Armed with explosive 40mm rounds, the Smelter is perfect for engaging enemy armour. The frontal blast shielding provides operators with ample cover during heated skirmishes.

fieldcannonw.name: Balfour Wolfhound 40mm
fieldcannonw.description: This destructive short-ranged cannon is designed for direct engagement with enemy fortifications. The Wolfhound is fitted with reinforced shielding and a 40mm barrel.

fieldhospital.name: Field Hospital
fieldhospital.description: A field medical facility used to treat critically wounded soldiers at the front line.

fieldmgc.name: G40 “Sagittarii”
fieldmgc.description: A dual barrelled, high calibre anti-infantry machine gun. The “Sagittarii” is fitted with forward-facing armour plating and is excellent for suppression.

fieldmgw.name: Swallowtail 988/127-2
fieldmgw.description: A dual barrelled, high calibre anti-infantry machine gun. The Swallowtail is fitted with forward-facing armour plating and is excellent for suppression.

fieldmortarc.name: 30-250 “Tisiphone” Field Cannon
fieldmortarc.description: The 30-250 “Tisiphone” Field Cannon is mobilized destruction incarnate. An infantry unit armed with these mortar cannons signals imminent devastation to enemy structures and emplacements.

fieldmortarw.name: Balfour Falconer 250mm
fieldmortarw.description: A heavy mobile mortar platform fitted with a thick frontal shield for assaulting fortified locations.

fieldpier.name: Navy Pier
fieldpier.description: A temporary pier that facilitates movement of goods and troops onto large naval vessels from the shore.

firepit.name: Fire Pit
firepit.description: A pit of fire used to keep soldiers warm during Snow Storms. Requires Diesel to fuel.

firepitt2.name: Field Range
firepitt2.description: This easy-to-construct range is perfect for keeping warm in the cold, while being sturdier than a simple firepit.

flatbedtruck.name: BMS - Packmule Flatbed
flatbedtruck.description: A heavy duty shipping transport truck designed by Bassett Motor Society. It’s built for hauling the heaviest of equipment over long distances with ease.

fortbaset1.name: Bunker Base
fortbaset1.description: A large forward operating base. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

fortbuildsite.description: A build site that serves as the foundation for constructing a bunker piece. Connects to other Bunkers and Trenches.

fortconstructionpart.name: Construction Parts
fortconstructionpart.description: The first piece of a Large Structure. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

fortcornerbuildsite.name: Bunker Corner Dig Site
fortcornerbuildsite.description: A build site that serves as the foundation for constructing a bunker corner piece. Connects to other Bunkers and Trenches.

fortcornert1.name: Bunker Corner (Tier 1)
fortcornert1.description: An underground shelter that serves as a foundation for permanent fortifications. Connects to other Bunkers and Trenches.

sandbags.description: A defensive barrier that hinders enemy movement and provides cover during combat.

strategicmap.description: A board fitted with various maps of the region and useful charts. Careful planning and navigation are the keys to any successful operation.

dishwashstation.description: Tidy up before a meal, wash clothes or clean dishes with a wash station. Hygiene is vital to avoid infection and disease.

latrine.description: A safe place for conducting business. After all, everyone has to.

kitchen.description: A safe, clean space to prepare rations or scavenged food supplies.

fortgarrisonstation.name: Underground Fortress
fortgarrisonstation.description: An underground strongpoint that can only be destroyed from explosives placed inside. Players can spawn and stockpile items here.

fortgarrisonstationpart.name: Underground Fortress Parts
fortgarrisonstationpart.description: The final critical components necessary to reinforce the basement of an Underground Fortress. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

fortintelcenterpart.name: Intelligence Center Parts
fortintelcenterpart.description: The final critical components necessary to construct a strategic Intelligence Center. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

fortlargeradar.name: SC-3 Aerial Interceptor Array
fortlargeradar.description: A radar that detects aircraft across a large effective radius and must be built atop a solid foundational hardpoint within bunker systems—for both operational stability and defence. The SC-3 Aerial Interceptor Array, or AIA, uses state-of-the-art technology to emit short radio bursts that provide aerial intelligence to nearby receivers and entrenched anti-aircraft defences.

fortlargeradarpart.name: Aircraft Radar Parts
fortlargeradarpart.description: The final critical components necessary to construct an Aircraft Radar. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

fortlrartillerypart.name: Storm Cannon Parts
fortlrartillerypart.description: The final critical components necessary to construct a powerful Storm Cannon. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

fortstructurepart.name: Structure Parts
fortstructurepart.description: The second piece of a Large Structure. Must be placed onto a Large Structure Foundation.

observationbunkert2.description: A radio bunker that provides intel to friendly units about the surrounding area. It is capable of increased effectiveness, and is equipped with binoculars that give occupants a greater field of view.

ammoroomt2.name: Storage Room (Tier 2)
ammoroomt2.description: A facility that stores equipment and ammunition for artillery. Retrieval is very quick for this structure.

engineroomt2.name: Engine Room (Tier 2)
engineroomt2.description: An engineering facility used to supply power to the whole Bunker.

fortflamet2.name: Fire Suppression Room (Tier 2)
fortflamet2.description: A bunker with a mechanical station that will automatically spray water in the event of a fire. Includes a pipe connection.
